entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company; domain context for this position on professional identity

entities.name: Semantic Identity
entities.context: Belief that professional identity is a collection of meaningful experiences (stories/truth) rather than fixed document forms

entities.name: Syntactic Rendering
entities.context: Document-level expression of identity; the exact words used in resumes, which can vary while truth remains constant

entities.name: Canonical Resume
entities.context: Rejected notion that there is one 'true' resume; position argues against this

entities.name: Career Coaching
entities.context: Practice that should shift focus from document polishing to story extraction

entities.name: AI
entities.context: Can handle syntactic rendering for different audiences once semantic core is captured

entities.name: Resume-Writing Bottleneck
entities.context: Problem in traditional career services that semantic approach eliminates

relationships.context: Core belief in Careerspan domain: identity is semantic, not syntactic
Semantic core (stories) is the source; syntactic forms are projections like 2D shadows of a 3D object
Focus should shift to extracting stories rather than polishing documents
AI handles rendering semantic core into various document forms for different audiences
Capturing semantic core eliminates the bottleneck of manual resume writing
Rejects the concept; argues there are only valid expressions of underlying experience
